title: Blessed Jesus at Thy Word

metadata.composer: 'Liebster Jesu wir Sind Hier' Johann Rudolph Ahle, 1664 Fridrich Layriz, 1854
metadata.lyricist: verses 1-3, Tobias Clausnitzer, 1663. verse 4, unknown author.  Translated by Catherine Winkworth, 1858

c_lines: Words: verses 1-3, Tobias Clausnitzer, 1663. verse 4, unknown author.  Translated by Catherine Winkworth, 1858.
Music: 'Liebster Jesu wir Sind Hier' Johann Rudolph Ahle, 1664.  Setting: Fridrich Layriz, 1854.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2012 Revision.

1.raw_text: Blessed Jesus, at Thy Word We are gathered all to hear Thee; Let our hearts and souls be stirred Now to seek and love and fear Thee, By Thy teachings sweet and holy, Drawn from earth to love Thee solely.

2.raw_text: All our knowledge, sense, and sight Lie in deepest darkness shrouded, Til Thy Spirit breaks our night With the beams of truth unclouded; Thou alone to God canst win us; Thou must work all good within us.

3.raw_text: Glorious Lord, Thyself impart! Light of light, from God proceeding, Open Thou our ears and heart; Help us by Thy Spirit's pleading; Hear the cry Thy people raises; Hear and bless our prayers and praises.

4.raw_text: Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, Praise to Thee and adoration! Grant that we Thy Word may trust And obtain true consolation, While we here below must wander, Till we sing Thy praises yonder.
